perturbation.original_text: Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro shall pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within thirty days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.
perturbation.changed_text: Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro *may* pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within sixty days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "shall" to "may", we introduce uncertainty regarding Aduro's obligation to pay the consultant. Originally, Aduro *shall* pay, indicating a definite obligation. Now, Aduro *may* pay, meaning payment is discretionary. Additionally the time frame for the payment was changed from 30 to 60 days. This contradicts with the entire section's intent and the certainty it provided before the change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation and Expenses. Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro *may* pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within sixty days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2. Invoices shall be sent to the attention of: ap@aduro.com Attn: Accounts Payable
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'shall' to 'may' regarding Aduro's payment obligation, introducing ambiguity and discretion where the original text and the contradicted text clearly establish a definite obligation. Additionally, the change from 'thirty' to 'sixty' days for payment directly conflicts with the established payment timeline, causing a strong in-text contradiction. This uncertainty in payment terms and timeline directly undermines the consultant's compensation as outlined in the original and contradicted text, making the payment terms inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. This adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction: Inconsistencies also lead to **in-text contradictions** when **different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties**.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time on prior written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement *only under special circumstances* on prior written notice to the other.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that either party can terminate the agreement at any time. By adding "only under special circumstances", we limit this freedom and create uncertainty about what qualifies as "special circumstances". This creates an in-text contradiction to the stated freedom and is not defined anywhere in the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall begin on the Effective Date and shall continue until December 31, 2020, unless extended or earlier terminated. Either party may terminate this Agreement *only under special circumstances* on prior written notice to the other. This Agreement may be extended upon mutual written agreement of the parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed for termination at any time. The changed text restricts this right to 'only under special circumstances'. The contradicted text repeats the change, solidifying the contradiction. This creates a clear contradiction because the original agreement allows for any time termination, while the changed agreement only allows for termination in special circumstances, which are not defined and therefore creates ambiguity and is a structural flaw as defined above. This is also an inconsistency as there is now conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns and transfers to Aduro, and to the extent that an executory assignment is not enforceable, Consultant hereby agrees to assign and transfer to Aduro, in writing, from time to time, upon request, any and all right, title, or interest that Consultant has or may obtain in any Work Product and/or Aduro IP without the necessity of further consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns and transfers to Aduro, and to the extent that an executory assignment is not enforceable, Consultant hereby agrees to assign and transfer to Aduro, in writing, from time to time, upon request, any and all right, title, or interest that Consultant has or may obtain in any Work Product and/or Aduro IP *subject to consultant's discretion* without the necessity of further consideration.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that the Consultant *irrevocably* assigns all rights to Work Product to Aduro. By adding "subject to consultant's discretion", we directly contradict this irrevocable assignment. Now, the consultant has the option to decide whether or not to transfer these rights, creating uncertainty about the ownership of Work Product. This is an in-text contradiction within the assignment clause itself. It is not specified, which rights can be reserved and which ones not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Aduro shall be the sole and exclusive owner of, and Consultant hereby assigns to Aduro, any and all writings, documents, work product, inventions, developments, improvements, discoveries, know-how, processes, chemical entities, compounds, plans, memoranda, tests, research, designs, specifications, models and data that Consultant makes, conceives, discovers or develops, either solely or jointly with any other person in performance of the Services (collectively, "Work Product"). Consultant shall promptly disclose to Aduro all information relating to Work Product as appropriate as part of the Services and at the request of Aduro. To the extent, if any, that Consultant has rights in or to any Work Product or any data or inventions developed in connection with work under this Agreement ("Aduro IP"), Consultant hereby irrevocably assigns and transfers to Aduro, and to the extent that an executory assignment is not enforceable, Consultant hereby agrees to assign and transfer to Aduro, in writing, from time to time, upon request, any and all right, title, or interest that Consultant has or may obtain in any Work Product and/or Aduro IP *subject to consultant's discretion* without the necessity of further consideration. Aduro shall be entitled to obtain and hold in its own name all copyrights, patents, trade secrets and trademarks with respect thereto. At Aduro's request and expense, Consultant shall assist Aduro in acquiring and maintaining its right in and title to, any Work Product. Such assistance may include, but will not be limited to, signing applications and other documents, cooperating in legal proceedings, and taking any other steps considered necessary or desirable by Aduro.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Consultant *irrevocably* assigns all rights to Work Product to Aduro, while the changed text adds "subject to consultant's discretion", directly contradicting this irrevocable assignment. This introduces a significant contradiction by giving the consultant the option to decide whether or not to transfer these rights, which conflicts with the first sentence of the contradicted text: "Aduro shall be the sole and exclusive owner of, and Consultant hereby assigns to Aduro, any and all writings, documents, work product..." Because it now allows the consultant to choose if they want to assign the rights, creating a clear and direct conflict and making the contract unenforceable.
